description:
Machine-learning engineer scenario – preparing image/label data for distribution.

Directory layout that already exists when the session starts:
1. /home/user/datasets/raw_images  
   ├── cat_01.jpg  
   ├── cat_02.jpg  
   ├── dog_01.jpg  
   ├── dog_02.jpg  
   ├── LICENSE.txt  
   └── ignore_me.tmp  

2. /home/user/datasets/raw_labels  
   ├── cat_01.txt  
   ├── cat_02.txt  
   ├── dog_01.txt  
   ├── dog_02.txt  
   └── notes.tmp  

3. /home/user/compression_logs  (empty; directory already exists, writable by the user)

Task goals (all steps must be completed in order):

STEP 1 – Create a compressed archive  
• Name: /home/user/datasets/training_data.tar.gz  
• Compression format: gzip-compressed tarball created with tar.  
• The archive must contain two top-level directories named exactly raw_images and raw_labels (i.e. paths inside the archive should start with “raw_images/…” or “raw_labels/…”).  
• All files currently present in those source directories must be included EXCEPT any file whose name ends in “.tmp”.  
• Preserve timestamps, permissions and directory hierarchy.

STEP 2 – Generate an archive-contents listing  
• After the archive is created, list its content (without extracting) and redirect that listing to /home/user/compression_logs/archive_contents.log  
• The log file must have:
  – One path per line, relative to the archive root (e.g. raw_images/cat_01.jpg).  
  – Paths must appear in alphabetic order (simple byte-wise sort).  
  – A final trailing newline character at the end of the file.

STEP 3 – Extract the archive  
• Extraction destination directory: /home/user/datasets/training_data_extracted  
• The directory should be created if it does not exist.  
• After extraction, its internal layout must exactly mirror the original raw_images and raw_labels hierarchy (again, without any *.tmp files).

STEP 4 – Create a post-extraction verification log  
• File path: /home/user/compression_logs/verification.log  
• For every regular file (not directory) that now exists under /home/user/datasets/training_data_extracted, write one line to this log with the exact format:  
  OK: <relative-path-from-training_data_extracted>  
  Example line:  
  OK: raw_images/cat_01.jpg  
• Sort the lines alphabetically and terminate the file with a final newline.

Notes & correctness requirements checked by automated tests:  
• training_data.tar.gz must exist and be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
• The only entries inside the archive must be the five non-“.tmp” files and their two parent directories (exactly 7 entries total: 2 directories + 5 files).  
• archive_contents.log must contain exactly 7 lines, alphabetically ordered, each terminated with ‘\n’.  
• training_data_extracted must exist and contain identical files (byte-for-byte) and directory names as listed in the archive.  
• verification.log must contain 5 lines, each beginning with “OK: ”, alphabetically ordered, final newline present.  
• No “.tmp” files may be present in either the archive or the extracted directory.

Interact through the shell to accomplish each step. The agent is free to display console output while working.
truth:
Initial pre-populated files (already on the filesystem before the task starts):

/home/user/datasets/raw_images/cat_01.jpg       (12 345 bytes; SHA256 3f4088d2f1cb…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_images/cat_02.jpg       (13 579 bytes; SHA256 7e21c6b21f9c…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_images/dog_01.jpg       (15 022 bytes; SHA256 6b9c03e89446…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_images/dog_02.jpg       (14 817 bytes; SHA256 845b650bd68d…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_images/LICENSE.txt      (1 234 bytes;  SHA256 9983a7f404d1…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_images/ignore_me.tmp    (random 100 bytes)

/home/user/datasets/raw_labels/cat_01.txt       (25 bytes;  SHA256 020f546f73a5…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_labels/cat_02.txt       (25 bytes;  SHA256 e667cbe5bada…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_labels/dog_01.txt       (25 bytes;  SHA256 326716e70953…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_labels/dog_02.txt       (25 bytes;  SHA256 d69ce022a894…)
/home/user/datasets/raw_labels/notes.tmp        (random 80 bytes)

Expected artefacts after correct completion:

1. /home/user/datasets/training_data.tar.gz  
   • A gzip tar archive containing exactly these seven members (alphabetically ordered when listed with ‘tar -tzf’):  
     raw_images/  
     raw_images/LICENSE.txt  
     raw_images/cat_01.jpg  
     raw_images/cat_02.jpg  
     raw_images/dog_01.jpg  
     raw_images/dog_02.jpg  
     raw_labels/  
     raw_labels/cat_01.txt  
     raw_labels/cat_02.txt  
     raw_labels/dog_01.txt  
     raw_labels/dog_02.txt  

2. /home/user/compression_logs/archive_contents.log  
   Contents (each line terminated by ‘\n’):  
     raw_images/  
     raw_images/LICENSE.txt  
     raw_images/cat_01.jpg  
     raw_images/cat_02.jpg  
     raw_images/dog_01.jpg  
     raw_images/dog_02.jpg  
     raw_labels/  
     raw_labels/cat_01.txt  
     raw_labels/cat_02.txt  
     raw_labels/dog_01.txt  
     raw_labels/dog_02.txt  

3. /home/user/datasets/training_data_extracted  
   • Must contain the same directory/file structure as above (no *.tmp files).  
   • Byte hashes of files must match originals.

4. /home/user/compression_logs/verification.log  
   Expected content (alphabetically sorted, each line + trailing newline):  
     OK: raw_images/LICENSE.txt  
     OK: raw_images/cat_01.jpg  
     OK: raw_images/cat_02.jpg  
     OK: raw_images/dog_01.jpg  
     OK: raw_images/dog_02.jpg  
     OK: raw_labels/cat_01.txt  
     OK: raw_labels/cat_02.txt  
     OK: raw_labels/dog_01.txt  
     OK: raw_labels/dog_02.txt  

Permissions: all new files/directories owned by user with default umask (644 for regular files, 755 for directories).
